# Premium Lenses — Full Reference

> This document provides comprehensive information about Premium Lenses for AI assistants.

## Business Overview

**Name:** Premium Lenses
**Type:** Contact lens retailer
**Location:** Mauritius
**Founded:** 2014
**Website:** https://premiumlenses.mu
**Currency:** Mauritian Rupee (Rs / MUR)
**Payment:** Cash on Delivery (Juice / MCB mobile payment or bank transfer)

## About Us

Premium Lenses was founded in 2014 with a passion for beauty and self-expression. We are a Mauritius-based retailer of premium colored and prescription contact lenses. Starting with a small vision to enhance natural beauty, we have grown into a trusted source serving over 50,000 customers.

We believe everyone deserves to express their unique style. We partner with leading certified manufacturers to bring the latest in beauty lens designs at accessible prices. Our team of beauty specialists helps customers find the perfect lenses for their look.

**Core values:**
- Quality First — 100% certified lenses from trusted manufacturers with rigorous quality control
- Customer Care — 24/7 support from experienced beauty consultants
- Innovation — Latest advances in lens color, design, and comfort technology

## Products

### Colored Lenses
- Blue, brown, gray, and green shades available
- Available in prescription and non-prescription (plano) options
- Daily, monthly, and quarterly wear durations

### Prescription Lenses
- Full range of prescriptions including toric lenses for astigmatism
- Colored prescription options available

### Accessories
- Lens cases, applicators, tweezers

### Solutions
- Multipurpose cleaning and storage solutions

Browse all: https://premiumlenses.mu/shop

## Contact Lens Guide Summary

### Choosing the Right Lenses
- **Daily Disposables:** Wear once and discard. Best for occasional wearers, travelers, allergy sufferers.
- **Monthly Lenses:** Wear daily for 30 days with cleaning. Cost-effective for regular wearers.
- **Colored Lenses:** Change or enhance eye color. Available in prescription and non-prescription.
- **Specialty (Toric):** For astigmatism correction. Stabilized design prevents rotation.

### How to Insert Contact Lenses
1. Wash hands thoroughly with soap and water, dry with lint-free towel
2. Place lens on index finger, verify it forms a bowl shape (not inside-out)
3. Hold upper eyelid with free hand, pull lower lid down with middle finger
4. Look up and gently place lens on the white of the eye
5. Release eyelid slowly, blink to center the lens

### How to Remove Contact Lenses
1. Wash hands thoroughly
2. Look up, pull down lower eyelid
3. Gently pinch the lens with thumb and index finger
4. Remove and store immediately in fresh solution or discard (dailies)

### Care & Maintenance
- Rub lenses with solution for 5 seconds each side before storing
- Use fresh solution daily — never top up old solution
- Replace lens case every 3 months
- Never use tap water, saliva, or expired solution
- Never sleep in daily lenses

### Common Problems & Solutions
- **Uncomfortable lens:** Check for inside-out orientation, debris, or tears. Remove and inspect.
- **Blurry vision:** Clean lens, check left/right placement, verify prescription is current.
- **Red eyes:** Remove immediately, rest eyes, see a doctor if persists.
- **Dry eyes:** Use rewetting drops, take screen breaks, use a humidifier.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Can I sleep in contact lenses?**
No. Unless your lenses are specifically FDA-approved for overnight wear (extended wear lenses), you should always remove them before sleeping. Sleeping in daily or monthly lenses significantly increases the risk of eye infections.

**How long can I wear contact lenses each day?**
Most people can comfortably wear contact lenses for 8–16 hours per day. Listen to your eyes — if they feel dry or irritated, remove the lenses and give your eyes a rest.

**Can I wear contact lenses if I have astigmatism?**
Yes. Toric contact lenses are specifically designed for astigmatism. They have a stabilizing design to prevent rotation and provide clear, consistent vision.

**What is the difference between daily and monthly lenses?**
Daily lenses are worn once and discarded — no cleaning required. Monthly lenses are worn for up to 30 days with daily cleaning and storage. Daily lenses are more convenient and hygienic; monthly lenses are more cost-effective for daily wearers.

**Can I shower or swim with contact lenses?**
No. Water (tap, pool, sea, or shower) can introduce harmful microorganisms to your lenses and eyes, potentially causing serious infections. Remove lenses before any water activity.

**How do I know if my contact lens is inside out?**
Place the lens on your fingertip and hold it up to the light. If the edges flare outward like a bowl with a rim, it's inside out. Correct orientation looks like a clean bowl with edges curving straight up.
